topic_name: Operation Bunyan Al Marsoos & Icube Qamar
subject: current-affairs
description: Operation Bunyan Al Marsoos & Icube Qamar MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: What is the name of the military operation launched by Pakistan in response to alleged Indian aggression, particularly after the Pahalgam terror attack?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's Armed Forces launched Operation Bunyan Al Marsoos as a direct response to Indian aggression following the Pahalgam terror attack and India's Operation Sindoor.

mcqs.q: India launched missile strikes against Pakistan under which operation?
mcqs.explanation: India launched Operation Sindoor as its cross-border military response following the Pahalgam attack.

mcqs.q: India launched 'Operation Sindoor' against _____ on 7 May 2025.
mcqs.explanation: India launched Operation Sindoor against Pakistan on 7 May 2025, prompting Pakistan's Operation Bunyan Al Marsoos three days later.

mcqs.q: Operation Bunyan Al Marsoos was launched by Pakistan on:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's Operation Bunyan Al Marsoos was launched on 10 May 2025; a US-brokered ceasefire was announced the same day.

mcqs.q: What does the word 'Bunyan' mean in Arabic?
mcqs.explanation: In Arabic, 'Bunyan' means a structure or building.

mcqs.q: What does the word 'Marsoos' mean in Arabic?
mcqs.explanation: 'Marsoos' means cemented or firmly joined together — a Quranic description of unity.

mcqs.q: In the Quran, 'Bunyan al Marsoos' refers to:
options: A garden beneath which rivers flow
A solid structure firmly joined together
A shining star in the sky
A promise of victory for the believers
mcqs.explanation: The Quran uses 'bunyan al marsoos' as a metaphor for believers fighting in Allah's cause as one solid structure firmly joined together.

mcqs.q: Why was the name 'Bunyan al Marsoos' chosen for Pakistan's military operation against India?
options: To highlight national unity and strong defence
To recall historical treaties with India
To honour ancient forts of the region
mcqs.explanation: The name highlights national unity and the solid, disciplined defence of Pakistan — drawing on the Quranic metaphor.

mcqs.q: The phrase 'Bunyan al Marsoos' appears in which Surah of the Holy Quran?
mcqs.explanation: The phrase appears in Surah Al-Saff, which is the 61st Surah of the Quran.

mcqs.q: Which verse of Surah Al-Saff contains the phrase 'Bunyan al Marsoos'?
mcqs.explanation: The phrase appears in Verse 4 of Surah As-Saff (61:4).

mcqs.q: Operation Azm-e-Istehkam was started on:
mcqs.explanation: Operation Azm-e-Istehkam — Pakistan's nationwide counter-terrorism campaign approved by the Apex Committee — was launched on 22 June 2024.

mcqs.q: Operation Sarbakaf was launched in Bajaur on:
mcqs.explanation: Operation Sarbakaf in Bajaur, KP was launched on 29 July 2025 as a local-area counter-terrorism operation.

mcqs.q: Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad was launched on:
mcqs.explanation: Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad, the nationwide successor to Zarb-e-Azb, was launched on 22 February 2017.

mcqs.q: Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad ended in:
mcqs.explanation: Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad formally concluded in November 2022, with counter-terrorism efforts later reframed under Azm-e-Istehkam (2024).

mcqs.q: Operation Khyber was launched on:
mcqs.explanation: Operation Khyber (the first phase of a series) was launched on 7 October 2014 in Khyber Agency.

mcqs.q: Operation Khyber ended on:
mcqs.explanation: Operation Khyber formally concluded on 21 August 2017 after multiple phases (Khyber I–IV).

mcqs.q: Operation Zarb-e-Azb was launched on:
mcqs.explanation: Operation Zarb-e-Azb was launched on 12 June 2014 after the Karachi Airport attack, targeting militant hideouts in North Waziristan.

mcqs.q: Operation Zarb-e-Azb ended on:
mcqs.explanation: Operation Zarb-e-Azb formally concluded on 22 February 2017 — the same day Radd-ul-Fasaad was launched.

mcqs.q: Operation Zarb-e-Azb was launched in:
mcqs.explanation: Operation Zarb-e-Azb's primary theatre was North Waziristan, the principal base of Tehreek-i-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) at the time.

mcqs.q: The Gaza offensive operation launched by Israel in May 2025 was codenamed:
mcqs.explanation: Israel codenamed its May 2025 Gaza offensive 'Operation Gideon's Chariots'.

mcqs.q: The EU Council decided to start Operation Aspides (Red Sea maritime protection) on:
mcqs.explanation: On 4 February 2024 (formally adopted on 19 February 2024), the Council of the European Union decided to launch Operation Aspides to protect commercial vessels in the Red Sea.

mcqs.q: Which country launched 'Operation Devil Hunt'?
mcqs.explanation: Operation Devil Hunt was announced and launched by Australia.

mcqs.q: Which country attacked Russia under 'Operation Web Spider'?
mcqs.explanation: Ukraine's operation codenamed 'Web Spider' targeted Russian air assets deep inside Russian territory.

mcqs.q: The active insurgent operation in Balochistan in June 2025 was codenamed:
mcqs.explanation: The active insurgent operation in Balochistan in June 2025 was codenamed Operation Shaheen.

mcqs.q: Iran's retaliatory operation against Israel (prior to True Promise 3) was named:
mcqs.explanation: Iran's major retaliatory strike on Israel was named 'Operation Glad Tidings of Victory' (True Promise 2).

mcqs.q: The US strikes on Fordow, Natanz and Isfahan nuclear facilities were codenamed:
mcqs.explanation: The US strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities at Fordow, Natanz and Isfahan in June 2025 were codenamed Operation Midnight Hammer.

mcqs.q: Iran's retaliatory operation following US strikes on its nuclear sites was codenamed:
mcqs.explanation: Iran launched Operation True Promise 3 in retaliation for the US 'Midnight Hammer' strikes on its nuclear facilities.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's first moon mission was named:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's first moon mission was named iCube Qamar (ICUBE-Q) — a CubeSat developed by IST Islamabad, SUPARCO and China's Shanghai Jiao Tong University.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's first moon mission was launched on:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's first moon mission iCube Qamar was launched on 3 May 2024.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's first moon mission was launched from Hainan city of:
mcqs.explanation: iCube Qamar was launched from Wenchang Space Launch Center on Hainan Island, China.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's first satellite mission to the moon, iCubeQamar, was launched on board China's:
mcqs.explanation: iCube Qamar was launched aboard China's Chang'e-6 — the world's first far-side lunar sample-return mission.

mcqs.q: The Pakistani ICUBE-Q CubeSat orbited the Moon as part of China's historic:
mcqs.explanation: ICUBE-Q orbited the Moon as part of China's Chang'e-6 far-side sample-return mission.

mcqs.q: The launch of the Chang'e-6 lunar mission was attended by scientists, diplomats and space-agency officials from:
mcqs.explanation: The Chang'e-6 launch hosted representatives from France, Italy, the European Space Agency (ESA) and Pakistan — all participants had payloads on the mission.

mcqs.q: On Chang'e-6, China carried payloads from:
mcqs.explanation: Chang'e-6 carried international payloads from France, Italy, Sweden and Pakistan.

mcqs.q: Chang'e-7 will carry payloads from Russia, Switzerland and Thailand when it launches in:
mcqs.explanation: China's Chang'e-7 lunar mission is scheduled for 2026 with payloads from Russia, Switzerland and Thailand.

mcqs.q: The 7 May 2025 Indian 'Operation Sindoor' was launched in response to which terror attack?
mcqs.explanation: India launched Operation Sindoor in response to the April 2025 Pahalgam terror attack in Indian-administered Kashmir.

mcqs.q: The India–Pakistan ceasefire in May 2025 was brokered mainly by:
mcqs.explanation: The ceasefire announcement on 10 May 2025 was brokered primarily through US diplomatic efforts and announced publicly by US President Donald Trump.

mcqs.q: Surah As-Saff — the source of the phrase 'bunyan al marsoos' — is which Surah in the Quran?
mcqs.explanation: Surah As-Saff is the 61st Surah of the Holy Quran.

mcqs.q: Operation Zarb-e-Azb was launched in response to which terrorist incident?
mcqs.explanation: The June 2014 Karachi (Jinnah) Airport attack was the immediate trigger for Operation Zarb-e-Azb against TTP hideouts in North Waziristan.

mcqs.q: The word 'Zarb-e-Azb' historically refers to the sword of:
mcqs.explanation: 'Zarb-e-Azb' means 'a sharp and cutting strike' and refers to one of the swords of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

mcqs.q: 'Radd-ul-Fasaad' in Arabic means:
mcqs.explanation: Radd-ul-Fasaad literally means 'elimination of discord/chaos'.

mcqs.q: Operation Azm-e-Istehkam was approved by the Central Apex Committee under which Prime Minister?
mcqs.explanation: Operation Azm-e-Istehkam was approved in June 2024 by the Central Apex Committee under PM Shehbaz Sharif's government.

mcqs.q: SUPARCO — Pakistan's space agency involved in iCube Qamar — was established in:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission (SUPARCO) was established in 1961.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's first-ever satellite (launched before iCube Qamar) was:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's first satellite was Badr-1, launched on 16 July 1990 — three-and-a-half decades before iCube Qamar reached the moon.

mcqs.q: Pakistan became the ______ country in the world to have a satellite mission to the Moon after iCube Qamar.
mcqs.explanation: With iCube Qamar on Chang'e-6, Pakistan became the 7th country overall to reach the Moon with a satellite (after USA, USSR/Russia, China, Japan, India and Israel).

mcqs.q: China's Chang'e-6 was historic because it:
options: Was the first human Moon mission
Returned the first-ever samples from the Moon's far side
mcqs.explanation: Chang'e-6 was the world's first mission to return lunar samples from the far side of the Moon (South Pole-Aitken basin).

mcqs.q: iCube Qamar was primarily developed by which Pakistani institute, with SUPARCO & Chinese collaboration?
mcqs.explanation: iCube Qamar was primarily developed by the Institute of Space Technology (IST) Islamabad, in collaboration with SUPARCO and Shanghai Jiao Tong University (SJTU), China.

mcqs.q: Operation Rising Lion (June 2025) was launched by:
mcqs.explanation: Operation Rising Lion was the Israeli military operation against Iran in June 2025 that preceded the US 'Operation Midnight Hammer' on Iran's nuclear sites.

mcqs.q: The Chang'e series of lunar missions is operated by the space agency of:
mcqs.explanation: The Chang'e programme of lunar missions is operated by the China National Space Administration (CNSA).

mcqs.q: Pakistan's iCube Qamar CubeSat carried which primary payload?
options: Two optical cameras for lunar imaging
mcqs.explanation: iCube Qamar carried two optical cameras designed to take images of the lunar surface from orbit.
